subject: Ecology
title: Urban Forests
description: A quick win: understand Urban Forests.

hook.text: Cities have forests too—parks, street trees, and backyard canopies that cool neighborhoods.

buildup.text: Urban trees reduce air pollution, lower summer temperatures, and cut stormwater runoff.

discovery.text: A mature city tree can intercept over 15,000 liters of rainwater per year.

twist.text: Wealthier neighborhoods often have 30% more tree cover than poorer ones in the same city.

climax.text: Planting urban forests is a public health intervention as much as an ecological one.

punchline.text: A city tree is infrastructure that breathes.

quiz.question: How do urban trees reduce flooding?
options: They heat the ground to evaporate water
